# Oodle Onboarding Examples

> Working examples showing how to integrate with [Oodle](https://oodle.ai), an observability platform for logs, metrics, and traces. Each example is self-contained with Docker Compose or Kubernetes manifests.

Every example follows the same pattern: `cp .env.example .env`, fill in credentials, `make up`.

## Choosing an Example

Pick by your current stack and what you want to send:

| Migrating from | Signal | Approach | Path |
|----------------|--------|----------|------|
| Elasticsearch | Logs | Dual-write (keep ES + add Oodle) | [elasticsearch-alternative/dual-write](elasticsearch-alternative/dual-write/README.md) |
| Elasticsearch | Logs | Single-write baseline (ES only) | [elasticsearch-alternative/single-write](elasticsearch-alternative/single-write/README.md) |
| OpenSearch | Logs | Dual-write (keep OS + add Oodle) | [opensearch-alternative/dual-write](opensearch-alternative/dual-write/README.md) |
| Datadog | Metrics, traces, logs | Dual-write (keep DD + add Oodle) | [datadog/dual-write](datadog/dual-write/README.md) |
| Datadog | Metrics, traces, logs | Single-write baseline (DD only) | [datadog/single-write](datadog/single-write/README.md) |
| None (greenfield) | Traces | Multi-language OTel tracing (Go, Java, Python) | [tracing-demo](tracing-demo/README.md) |
| None (greenfield) | Traces | LLM observability (Traceloop + Gemini) | [traceloop-demo](traceloop-demo/README.md) |
| None (Kubernetes) | Traces, metrics, logs | Zero-code auto-instrumentation via OTel Operator | [k8s-otel-operator](k8s-otel-operator/README.md) |

## Log Collection Agents

The Elasticsearch and OpenSearch migration examples support multiple agents. Pick the one matching your existing pipeline:

- **Fluent Bit** -- Lightweight forwarder. Add an Oodle HTTP output alongside the existing output.
- **Vector** -- High-performance pipeline. Add an Oodle HTTP sink alongside the existing sink.
- **OTel Collector** -- OpenTelemetry native. Add an Oodle OTLP exporter alongside the existing exporter.
- **Filebeat + Logstash** -- Classic Elastic stack. Add an Oodle HTTP output in the Logstash pipeline. (Elasticsearch only)

Switch agents with `make up AGENT=fluent-bit|vector|otel|logstash`.

## Detailed Example Descriptions

### elasticsearch-alternative/dual-write

Migrate from Elasticsearch to Oodle. Runs a local ES stack (Elasticsearch + Kibana) with a Go demo app emitting structured JSON logs. A log collection agent (Fluent Bit, Vector, OTel Collector, or Filebeat+Logstash) writes to both Elasticsearch and Oodle simultaneously.

- Languages: Go
- Infra: Docker Compose
- Signals: Logs
- Agents: Fluent Bit, Vector, OTel Collector, Filebeat+Logstash
- Config files: `agents/fluent-bit/fluent-bit.conf`, `agents/vector/vector.yaml`, `agents/otel/otel-collector-config.yaml`, `agents/filebeat/filebeat.yml`, `agents/logstash/pipeline/logstash.conf`
- Oodle onboarding guide: [OODLE_ONBOARDING.md](elasticsearch-alternative/dual-write/OODLE_ONBOARDING.md)

### elasticsearch-alternative/single-write

Baseline Elasticsearch-only setup (no Oodle). Use as a starting point before enabling dual-write.

- Languages: Go
- Infra: Docker Compose
- Signals: Logs
- Agents: Fluent Bit, Vector, OTel Collector

### opensearch-alternative/dual-write

Migrate from OpenSearch to Oodle. Runs a local OpenSearch stack (OpenSearch + Dashboards) with a Go demo app. A log collection agent writes to both OpenSearch and Oodle simultaneously.

- Languages: Go
- Infra: Docker Compose
- Signals: Logs
- Agents: Fluent Bit, Vector, OTel Collector (via Data Prepper)
- Config files: `agents/fluent-bit/fluent-bit.conf`, `agents/vector/vector.yaml`, `agents/otel/otel-collector-config.yaml`
- Oodle onboarding guide: [OODLE_ONBOARDING.md](opensearch-alternative/dual-write/OODLE_ONBOARDING.md)

### datadog/dual-write

Dual-ship from Datadog Agent to both Datadog and Oodle. Uses Datadog's native `DD_ADDITIONAL_ENDPOINTS` support -- no application code changes. A Go demo app sends custom metrics (DogStatsD), APM traces, and structured logs through the Datadog Agent.

- Languages: Go
- Infra: Docker Compose
- Signals: Metrics, traces, logs (all three)
- Agent: Datadog Agent with dual-shipping config
- Requires: Datadog API key + Oodle credentials

### datadog/single-write

Baseline Datadog-only setup (no Oodle). Same Go demo app sending metrics, traces, and logs through the Datadog Agent.

- Languages: Go
- Infra: Docker Compose
- Signals: Metrics, traces, logs
- Agent: Datadog Agent
- Requires: Datadog API key

### tracing-demo

End-to-end distributed tracing across four microservices in three languages, with automatic load generation.

- Languages: Go (frontend-api, billing-service), Java Spring Boot (order-service), Python Flask (inventory-service)
- Infra: Docker Compose
- Signals: Traces
- Instrumentation: OTel SDK (Go, Python), OTel Java Agent (auto-instrumentation)
- Collector: OTel Collector exporting to Oodle via OTLP
- Oodle onboarding guide: [OODLE_ONBOARDING.md](tracing-demo/OODLE_ONBOARDING.md)

### traceloop-demo

LLM observability using Traceloop's OpenLLMetry SDK. A Python Flask app calls Google Gemini and exports traces (including token usage, latency, request/response content) to Oodle via OTel Collector.

- Languages: Python
- Infra: Docker Compose
- Signals: Traces (LLM-specific)
- Instrumentation: Traceloop SDK (auto-instruments Gemini calls)
- Collector: OTel Collector exporting to Oodle via OTLP
- Requires: Google Gemini API key + Oodle credentials
- Oodle onboarding guide: [OODLE_ONBOARDING.md](traceloop-demo/OODLE_ONBOARDING.md)

### k8s-otel-operator

Zero-code distributed tracing on Kubernetes using the OpenTelemetry Operator. The operator injects instrumentation automatically via pod annotations -- no OTel SDK imports in application code.

- Languages: Node.js (frontend), Python (order-service), Java (payment-service)
- Infra: Kubernetes (minikube), OTel Operator, cert-manager
- Signals: Traces, metrics, logs
- Instrumentation: OTel Operator auto-injection (inject-nodejs, inject-python, inject-java annotations)
- Collector: Operator-managed OTel Collector exporting to Oodle via OTLP

## Oodle Configuration

All examples need two environment variables:

```
OODLE_INSTANCE=your-instance-id
OODLE_API_KEY=your-api-key
```

Get these from the Oodle UI: Settings > API Keys.

### Oodle Ingest Endpoints

| Signal | Protocol | Endpoint Pattern |
|--------|----------|-----------------|
| Logs | HTTP/JSON | `https://{INSTANCE}-logs.collector.oodle.ai/ingest/v1/logs` |
| Logs | OTLP/HTTP | `https://{INSTANCE}-logs.collector.oodle.ai/ingest/otel/v1/logs` |
| Traces | OTLP/HTTP | `https://{INSTANCE}-otlp.collector.oodle.ai/v1/traces` |
| Metrics (Datadog) | Datadog API | `https://{COLLECTOR}/v1/datadog/{INSTANCE}` |

### Authentication Headers

```
X-OODLE-INSTANCE: {OODLE_INSTANCE}
X-API-KEY: {OODLE_API_KEY}
```
